visa_policy.notes: The official visa-exempted countries page lists nationalities whose citizens do not require a pre-entry visa. They receive a Visitors Permit on arrival valid for up to 4 months (extendable once by 2 months, for a maximum of 6 months total). The 4-month period is treated as approximately 120 days for record-keeping; the official page states '4 months' not a precise day count. No e-visa or ETA system is operated by Fiji. All other nationalities must obtain a Single Entry Visa (FJD 93, valid 3 months) or Multiple Entry Visa (FJD 185, valid 12 months per entry up to 4 months) prior to travel. Fiji does not operate a visa-on-arrival scheme; visa-exempt nationals receive a visitors permit on arrival (not a purchased visa). Cook Islands nationals travel on NZ passports and are therefore listed under conditional_access, not visa_free.

cbi.notes: Fiji does not have a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) programme. Citizenship is only obtainable by birth, registration, or naturalisation (requiring lawful residency under the Citizenship of Fiji Act 2009). Dual citizenship is permitted per the official Ministry of Immigration website. Source for dual citizenship: https://www.immigration.gov.fj/fiji-citizenship/

programs.name: Three-Year Investor Permit
programs.notes: Issued to non-citizen investors engaged in a business project approved by the Fiji Trade and Investment Bureau (FTIB) with a Foreign Investment Certificate (FIC). Applicable to investments under F$500,000. The exact minimum threshold is not stated on immigration.gov.fj; the page requires FTIB approval and FIC but does not specify a dollar floor. Permit is valid for 3 years. Does not directly confer permanent residency; path to citizenship is through naturalisation under the Citizenship of Fiji Act 2009 after meeting residency requirements.

programs.name: Seven-Year Investor Permit
programs.notes: For investments of F$500,000 or more with an approved business trade or undertaking. The investor must introduce and maintain capital or assets of at least F$500,000, provide proof of available funds as personal capital (not unsecured loans), own or hold a lease/purchase option for required land or buildings, and demonstrate funds transferred from overseas. Permit is valid for 7 years. Path to citizenship is through naturalisation. Note: the previously recorded URL (immigration.gov.fj/index.php/seven-7-years-investor-permit) appears to redirect to the three-year permit page; updated to point to the main investor permit page.

conditional_access.conditions: Cook Islands nationals do not hold their own internationally-issued passport; they travel on New Zealand passports. As NZ passport holders they are visa-exempt and receive a visitors permit on arrival for up to 4 months. The exemption is listed on the official page as 'Cook Islands [NZ passport holder]'.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: max_stay_days set to 120 (approx 4 months) for all visa_free entries - previously null. The Visitors Permit page confirms 'up to a maximum of four months'; 120 days used as the standard approximation.
CORRECTED: Paraguay iso3 was null - corrected to PRY.
CORRECTED: Peru iso3 was null - corrected to PER.
CORRECTED: Macao iso3 was null - corrected to MAC (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3).
CORRECTED: Bermuda iso3 was null - corrected to BMU (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 for the British Overseas Territory).
CORRECTED: Hong Kong (SAR) iso3 was null - corrected to HKG (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3).
CORRECTED: conditional_access Cook Islands entry max_stay_days set to 120 (was null), consistent with all other visa-free entries.

CORRECTED: Seven-Year Investor Permit official_url updated - the previously recorded URL (immigration.gov.fj/index.php/seven-7-years-investor-permit) redirects to the three-year permit page; updated to main investor permit page.

REMOVED: fijiembassy.jp from official_domains - this is a diplomatic post website; its content is secondary to the Ministry of Immigration's own pages and was used only to corroborate the F$500,000 threshold, which is not stated on immigration.gov.fj itself.

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Visa-exempted countries list re-fetched live from immigration.gov.fj/visa-exempted-countries/ - all 112 visa_free entries in this file are confirmed present on the official page. No additions, removals, or reclassifications required.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: No e-visa, ETA, or visa-on-arrival scheme exists - re-confirmed on fiji-visas page (only SEV FJD 93 and MEV FJD 185 available via ImmiHub portal; no automated ETA issued).

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Visitors permit extension - 'maximum of four months' initial stay, extendable once by 2 months (total max 6 months), extension fee FJD 93. Processing 3–5 working days at Suva office. Confirmed from visitors-permit page.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: No CBI program - confirmed from fiji-citizenship page. Dual citizenship permitted under Citizenship Act.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Cook Islands conditional_access entry correctly classified - official page explicitly notes '(NZ passport holders only)' for Cook Islands.

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Investor permit page (immigration.gov.fj/investor-permit-for-non-citizen-investors/) currently does not separately distinguish 3-year vs 7-year tiers or state minimum investment amounts explicitly; only FTIB approval requirement listed. Pre-existing gap confirmed as still open.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Visa fees confirmed - SEV FJD 93 (valid 3 months, single entry), MEV FJD 185 (valid 12 months, multiple entries, max 4 months per stay). Processing 14 working days.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Macao listed under Americas/Caribbean section on official page (unusual grouping) but correctly recorded as visa_free with iso3 MAC.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-fetch of immigration.gov.fj/visa-exempted-countries/ confirmed. All visa_free nationality entries match the live official page. Taiwan (TWN) confirmed present under Asia section (was present in current file; an earlier AI extraction artefact had missed it — confirmed on targeted re-fetch). Cook Islands correctly in conditional_access (NZ passport holders only). 'Commonwealth Dominica' listed under Africa on the official page is a duplicate of Dominica (DMA) in Americas — not added as a separate entry.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: Germany display name updated from 'Germany' to 'The Federal Republic of Germany' to match exact wording on the official immigration.gov.fj/visa-exempted-countries/ page. iso3 DEU unchanged.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: No ETA/e-visa system introduced. No visa-on-arrival scheme. SEV (FJD 93) and MEV (FJD 185) remain the only visa categories for non-exempt nationals. Visitor permit maximum 4 months (extendable once by 2 months, FJD 93 fee). All confirmed from official pages re-fetched 2026-06-29.
gaps: The official visa-exempted countries page does not specify individual max_stay_days per nationality; 120-day figure is derived from the Visitors Permit page which states 'up to 4 months'.

The exact minimum investment threshold for the Three-Year Investor Permit (F$50,000 cited by non-official aggregators) could not be confirmed verbatim on immigration.gov.fj; the page states only that FTIB approval is required without specifying a dollar floor. Still unconfirmed as of 2026-06-27 re-check.

The Seven-Year Investor Permit dedicated page (immigration.gov.fj/index.php/seven-7-years-investor-permit) appears to redirect to the three-year page; the F$500,000 threshold for the 7-year permit is not independently confirmable from the current immigration.gov.fj page alone. Still unconfirmed as of 2026-06-27 re-check.
No official e-visa or ETA system was found for Fiji; online applications exist via ImmiHub portal (immihub.immigration.gov.fj) but these are standard visa applications, not automated authorizations.
No digital nomad visa, startup visa, or formal fast-track skilled worker scheme was found on official sources.
No formal freedom-of-movement agreement (e.g. Pacific-wide) applicable to Fiji nationals was found on official sources.

Niue and Tokelau nationals (NZ-associated territories) are not explicitly listed on the official page - they likely travel on NZ passports and would be captured by the NZL visa_free entry, but this is not explicitly confirmed.

Moldova (MDA) and Georgia (GEO) are listed verbatim on the official immigration.gov.fj visa-exempted countries page and have been retained; their presence is unusual but confirmed from the live official source as of 2026-06-27.

outbound_official.note: no official Fiji government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Both foreignaffairs.gov.fj (Ministry of Foreign Affairs) and immigration.gov.fj (Ministry of Immigration) focus exclusively on inbound travel to Fiji. The MFA publishes individual country travel advisories (e.g. Middle East warnings) but no comprehensive list of where Fiji ordinary-passport holders may travel visa-free, visa-on-arrival, or via e-visa/ETA. The immigration.gov.fj 'Departing Fiji' page covers health, customs, and documentation guidance only - no visa-access matrix for Fiji citizens.

visa_types.name: Visitors Permit (Visa-Exempt Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Allows nationals of visa-exempt countries to enter Fiji for tourism, visiting friends/family, attending events, conferences, or transiting. Issued on arrival without prior application.
visa_types.notes: Only available to nationals of visa-exempt countries listed at immigration.gov.fj/visa-exempted-countries/. Valid for up to 4 months (approx. 120 days). Extendable once by 2 months (FJD 93 fee) for a total maximum stay of 6 months. Business visitors on 30-day permits cannot extend. Cannot be used for employment.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (SEV)
visa_types.purpose: Allows nationals of non-visa-exempt countries to enter Fiji once for tourism, business visits, training, transit, crew travel, or government duties. Must be obtained before travel.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 93.00 (approx. USD 42 at current rates; exact USD equivalent varies). Valid for 3 months from issue date. Processing takes approximately 14 working days. Applications lodged at any Fijian Immigration office in Suva, Nadi, Lautoka, Savusavu, Levuka, Labasa, and Rotuma, or through nearest Fiji High Commission/Embassy abroad. Online portal (ImmiHub) also available.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (MEV)
visa_types.purpose: Allows nationals of non-visa-exempt countries to make repeated visits to Fiji over a 12-month period for tourism, business, training, transit, crew travel, or government duties.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 185.00 (approx. USD 84 at current rates; exact USD equivalent varies). Valid for 12 months from issue date; maximum stay per entry is 4 months (approx. 120 days). Processing takes approximately 14 working days. Applications lodged at Fijian Immigration offices or nearest Fiji High Commission/Embassy abroad.

visa_types.name: Business Visitor Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to visit Fiji for short-term business activities such as meetings, conferences, and negotiations without engaging in local employment.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 30-day periods. Cannot be extended. Available to visa-exempt nationals on arrival; visa-required nationals must obtain a SEV or MEV first. No paid employment permitted.

visa_types.name: Long-Term Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens employed by an overseas employer or holding a local job offer to work in Fiji for periods exceeding one year.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 650.05 (general) or FJD 475.35 (religious purposes). Standard processing is 21 working days; may vary based on application completeness and background checks. Applicants must be over 18, hold a valid passport with 3+ years remaining, and have a signed employment contract. Spouse and children under 18 may be included. Extension applications must be lodged 1 month prior to expiry. Apply via ImmiHub portal at immihub.immigration.gov.fj.

visa_types.name: Short-Term Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens to work in Fiji for one year or less under a specific employment contract with an overseas or local employer.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 650.05. Work period is one year or less; permit cannot be extended as short-term - only upgradeable to long-term permit. No dependents permitted. Employment changes require immigration approval. Applicants must be over 18. Apply via ImmiHub portal.

visa_types.name: Maritime and Fishing Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens to work aboard fishing vessels operating in Fijian waters, covering positions such as captain, engineer, deckhand, cook, seaman, and shore-based management staff.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 650.05 for new and extension applications; FJD 664.50 for processing/renewal. Applicants must be over 18. A Contract of Employment is mandatory. Extension applications must be lodged 1 month prior to expiry. Contact: fidpermits@gmail.com.

visa_types.name: NGO Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens to work in Fiji for registered non-governmental organisations.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, validity, and processing details not published on the main immigration site. Apply via ImmiHub portal or contact fidpermits@gmail.com.

visa_types.name: Secondment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows employees of overseas companies to be seconded to a Fijian company or organisation for a defined period.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, validity, and processing details not published on the main immigration site. Apply via ImmiHub portal.

visa_types.name: Student Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorises non-citizens to study at secondary, tertiary, vocational institutions, or universities in Fiji. Not available for primary school enrolment.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 321.00; additional bond fees apply after approval. Standard processing is 21 working days. Requires acceptance letter for current academic year, proof of financial support or scholarship documentation, medical report (under 3 months old), and local guardian/sponsor letter. Local police report required if applicant is over 18 and has studied 12+ months in Fiji. Extensions must be lodged 1 month before expiry. Apply via ImmiHub portal.

visa_types.name: Internship/Training Attachment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens to undertake internships or training attachments at Fijian organisations or institutions.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, validity, and processing details not published on the main immigration site. Apply via ImmiHub portal.

visa_types.name: Research Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens to conduct academic or scientific research in Fiji.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, validity, and processing details not published on the main immigration site. Apply via ImmiHub portal.

visa_types.name: Three-Year Investor Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens who have an approved business investment project (under FJD 500,000) approved by the Fiji Trade and Investment Bureau (FTIB) to reside in Fiji as investors.
visa_types.notes: Fee is FJD 650.05 for new and extension applications. Valid for 3 years. Requires Foreign Investment Certificate (FIC) from FTIB, company registration, tax ID, proof of funds, police report, and medical report. Spouse and dependent children can be included. Path to citizenship only through naturalisation. Exact minimum investment threshold not stated on official site; FTIB approval required.

visa_types.name: Seven-Year Investor Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens investing FJD 500,000 or more in an approved business in Fiji to reside in Fiji as long-term investors.
visa_types.notes: For investments of FJD 500,000 or more. Investor must introduce and maintain capital/assets of at least FJD 500,000 as personal capital (not unsecured loans), own or hold a lease/purchase option on required land or buildings, and demonstrate funds transferred from overseas. Valid for 7 years. Path to citizenship through naturalisation only.

visa_types.name: Permit on Assured Income
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign retirees with a guaranteed foreign income or sufficient overseas assets to reside in Fiji on a long-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, minimum income threshold, validity period, and detailed processing information were not available on the official immigration.gov.fj website at time of research. Apply via ImmiHub portal or contact fidpermits@gmail.com for details.

visa_types.name: Co-Extensive Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows dependents (spouse and children) of permit holders to reside in Fiji for the same duration as the primary permit holder.
visa_types.notes: Issued to dependents co-extensive with the primary permit holder's permit (work, investor, student, etc.). Validity mirrors the primary permit. Specific fee details not listed separately on official site.

visa_types.name: Special Purpose Permit (Yacht Owners and Crew)
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-citizens who are yacht owners or crew to reside aboard their yacht while in Fijian waters.
visa_types.notes: Permit becomes null and void once the yacht exits Fiji. Yacht must be legally registered in Fiji. Requires an approved cruising permit from the Ministry of I-Taukei Affairs. Co-extensive permits available for spouses and dependents. Specific fee details not published.

visa_types.name: Transit Visitors Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows travellers to transit through Fiji for more than three hours while in transit to another destination.
visa_types.notes: Transit exceeding three hours requires a visitors permit. For visa-exempt nationals this is granted on arrival. Non-visa-exempt nationals transiting must hold a valid SEV or MEV. Specific transit-only visa category is not separately defined; transit is listed as one of the eligible purposes under the standard visitors permit/visa framework.
